name: May 2026 VLCC PG → Singapore
label: Editable analyst scenario, not live market data
description: Default scenario for the VLCC Route Lab. Compares Cape of Good Hope vs Suez Canal routing for a Persian Gulf to Singapore VLCC voyage. Values are summarised in our own words from an analyst brief and are EDITABLE in the dashboard. Do not treat as live market data.
notes: PG → Singapore is a non-EEA → non-EEA voyage; EU ETS coverage is 0%.
Cape congestion delay of 6 days can be used as a stress test (set in the dashboard).
Insurance percentages are placeholders — real AWRP varies by underwriter, hull, and current risk reports.

sources.citation: IPCC default emission factor for residual fuel oil (~3.114 t CO2 per t fuel).

European Commission, 'FAQ — Maritime transport in the EU Emissions Trading System (ETS)'; EMSA Maritime ETS resources; Directive (EU) 2023/959. Coverage is 100% for EEA↔EEA, 50% for EEA↔non-EEA, 0% for non-EEA↔non-EEA voyages, with 100% berth emissions in EEA ports.
Suez Canal Authority toll circulars (SCNT-based). Verify with the latest circular for the vessel's tonnage and direction.
Bunker price feed NOT configured in this build. Replace via providers.py once a real source is wired (Ship & Bunker, Argus, Platts, etc.).
TCE / charter rate feed NOT configured. Verify against Baltic Exchange / Clarksons / Argus.
Underwriter / war-risk broker quote. Premiums move with current threat assessments and JWC listed-areas.
